scope.setting: finite noisy unital one-qubit Pauli-diagonal observer-bridge channels
scope.probe_family: full-rank antipodal qubit states (I +/- rP)/2 for P in {X,Y,Z}
scope.claim_boundary: finite Pauli-channel theorem/certificate; not continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter, dS/CFT, or type-III algebra

core_claim: Algebraic connectivity is measured by approximate recoverable observer algebra: relative-entropy response plus product/commutator closure separates quantum, classical, and null bridge phases while static entropy shadows remain blind.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: For a nonnegative Pauli-diagonal bridge, if all three Pauli-axis relative-entropy defects are at most epsilon, each axis shrink is at least mu and the bridge has explicit fidelity and product/commutator stability bounds.
Three stable noncommuting axes with closure define a quantum bridge; one stable commuting axis defines a classical bridge; no stable axis defines a null bridge.
Axis-wise response alone can preserve two noncommuting probes while missing their commutator axis, so product/commutator closure is necessary.

simulation_signature.proposal: Prepare full-rank antipodal probe pairs for noncommuting observer operators in a random-circuit, tensor-network, or quantum-simulator bridge; estimate relative-entropy response and product/commutator closure.
simulation_signature.falsifiable_signal: same coarse entropy/static shadow, different algebraic connectivity phase

known_vs_new.known_substrate: OA-QEC, relative entropy recovery, universal recovery bounds, and finite preserved-information algebra structure.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Defines and certifies a finite noisy bridge order parameter based on recoverable observer algebra; records entropy-blind quantum/classical/null transitions and a response-only closure no-go.

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 19 algebraic connectivity order-parameter certificate.
Run focused regression for the Goal 19 certificate.
Validate the machine-readable Goal 19 certificate index.

limitations: Finite noisy Pauli-channel theorem/certificate only. The next lift is a general finite-dimensional OA-QEC stability theorem beyond Pauli-diagonal bridges.
